seo.title: Ginger Beer - just for summer - Marinda Kook

html:
Making ginger beer is not a difficult process – you just need time and the sun on your side. If you get up one Saturday morning, you can knock it together quickly and by that evening you're sitting back drinking your ginger beer. It's so lovely watching the raisins swell and rise to the top in the bottle. You'll feel like a cat that got the cream.

You need a bucket with a tightly sealing lid. And start collecting all your empty plastic cooldrink bottles.

YOU'LL NEED:

  • 7 x 750 ml lukewarm water
  • 5 x 250 ml white sugar
  • 10 ml Lennons Jamaica ginger (buy it at your supermarket or the pharmacy)
  • 10 ml instant yeast (1 packet)
  • 40 raisins

NOW:

  1. Mix the instant yeast and 10 ml sugar in a container.
  2. Pour 125 ml lukewarm water onto the sugar/yeast mixture. Leave it to stand. After about 10 to 20 minutes it will start to bubble.
  3. In your bucket, mix the lukewarm water and the sugar. Stir it until the sugar has dissolved.
  4. Now stir the Jamaica ginger into the bucket too.
  5. Lastly, mix the yeast/sugar mixture into the bucket.
  6. Add 40 raisins to the water mixture.
  7. Put the lid on the bucket and leave the bucket in the sun for at least 6 hours. You'll see it start to ferment.
  8. Let the air out of the bucket every now and then, otherwise the lid will pop off.
  9. Bottle your ginger beer and put a few raisins into each bottle.
  10. Put the bottles in the fridge to stop the fermentation process.

Ginger beer is deliciously refreshing and guaranteed not to give you a hangover. Use it with alcoholic drinks too if you do want a hangover! Delicious over ice with a splash of brandy or whisky!
